paragraphs: Collapse. That's what'll happen to the Northern Alliance on Sol. A structural failure. One day, they were the gatekeepers to humanity's next dawn.[#pause] The next, they were like three billion insects, realizing the lid closed. They were, once again, trapped in their jar. The watch.[#pause2]
Composure requires integrity. That, Bellamy understood. She, at least, had stayed on her feet in the moment of her contact. Her First Contact.[#pause], I did not.
First Contact rearranges you. Your brain. Literally. Ask Joana. Ask Su.

sections.heading:
31 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
SECTOR 4 · FREYA HOMESTEAD
paragraphs: Lysanne met Oliver at the service junction. Ian was already waiting, arms folded. Next to him stood a woman Lysanne didn't know. She wore a bright white lab coat and stood completely still, watching everything closely. Her black hair was drawn back in a quick, practical knot, but a few loose strands fell across her tired, still deeply focused face.

paragraphs.text: "[low]Lysanne Sutherland," Ian said, "[low]this is Joana. My wife."
paragraphs.speaker: ian-perera

paragraphs: Joana looked at her with the calm assessment of someone who had already heard about her and was comparing notes.

paragraphs.text: "[measured]He speaks highly of you," Joana said.
paragraphs.speaker: joana-perera

paragraphs.text: Lysanne shook her hand. "[dry]Then he's easily impressed."
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs: Joana's mouth moved. Almost a smile. Lysanne decided she liked her immediately, which was unusual. She didn't usually like people immediately.

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]In two clicks," Oliver whispered, his voice barely rising above the conduits' hum, "[whispered]there'll be a major event. It'll be our only chance."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: *Our.*

paragraphs: Lysanne looked at him.

paragraphs.text: *When had it become our?*
"[flat]I'll be nowhere near the depot that day," she said.
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[pressing]Can't you change it?" Oliver pressed.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[wry]You think I can rewrite the work roster?"
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: *Naive boy.*
"[wry]We go where the Grid tells us to go."
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs: She paused.

paragraphs.text: "[curious]And what major event?"
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]The *Brisinger*," he whispered. "[whispered]That's when she'll descend. While everyone is looking up, I'll go to the depot, get the Egg, and use it to disappear from the Grid. Permanently."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: Lysanne crossed her arms. "[skeptical]So what if you disappear from the Grid? You'll have nowhere to go. Nothing to eat or drink."
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]I'll figure it out," he said. "[quiet]Maybe."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: *Maybe. He said* maybe*.*
"[scoffing]Maybe?!" she scoffed. "[firm]That won't fly, Olly. Don't be stupid."
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]Maybe I'm catching a ride to the Delta."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[disbelieving]The Delta?!"
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs: The disbelief in her voice was absolute and entirely genuine.

paragraphs.text: "[disbelieving]How in the void?"
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs: Oliver glanced at Ian, then back at her.

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]Someone reached me," he said quietly. "[quiet]Through the civilian network. They know about the Egg."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: He paused.

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]They're going to extract me during the *Brisinger*'s approach window."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: The conduits hummed. Lysanne looked at Ian. Ian looked at the floor, which was its own kind of answer.

paragraphs.text: "[pressing]Help me reach the depot," Oliver continued. "[pressing]Get me as close as you can."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[wary]It's a monitored sector."
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[reasoning]They won't care about a random blip near a scrapyard when they have a giant falling on their doorstep."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: His voice echoed, and he hushed his next words.

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]If you can get me there, I can do the rest."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: *Can you, though?*
She looked at him and sighed. "[resigned]Fine. I'll get you there. You remember which vent I left unlocked?"
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[low]Yes."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: Then Joana spoke for the first time since the handshake.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]There's something else. I've found Commander Vilde. She's being held in the stasis bay."
paragraphs.speaker: joana-perera

paragraphs.text: *Of course there's something else,*

paragraphs: Lysanne thought.

paragraphs.text: *First one filter, then a stupid Egg, and now we're apparently running a resistance. Fantastic. Great. Absolutely fine.*

sections.heading:
33 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
CRYO BAY · FREYA HOMESTEAD
paragraphs: A blond boy floated in the pale blue cryo-gel. Joana peered through the reinforced glass, her brow furrowed in concentration. The blond boy, a severe EDS patient, lay gently in suspended animation.

Her gaze drifted past the boy's pod to the rest of the bay. A black screen hid the other bays from view. Only one sealed door, marked with a white fox, allowed entry. A guardian stood guard next to it. At first, he had watched her every move. But now, bored, he mostly fidgeted with his slate. It [#chime]chimed a series of urgent pings.
He read the incoming directive.

paragraphs.text: "[clipped]Emergency. I've been called to the docks, and I can't leave you here. You need to go."
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs.text: "[concerned]But the boy," Joana began, feigning professional concern. "[concerned]The boy can't be left unattended. I'm in the middle of a treatment!"
paragraphs.speaker: joana-perera

paragraphs: The guardian froze, his hand halfway to the door panel. He was torn between the urgent summons and his duty to guard Joana. His slate [#alert-pong]beeped again, even more urgently. He looked from Joana's steady gaze to the boy in the cryo-gel, clearly weighing his options.

paragraphs.text: "[firm]Your work is restricted to this bay. Don't touch anything or go anywhere until I get back," he said.
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs: Joana nodded. He tapped the door panel with his wristband. The heavy [#doorhiss]door closed. He was gone. She was alone. This was her chance. She opened the bay's schematic on her slate and highlighted a maintenance panel hidden behind a supply unit. It led to the emergency coolant purge system, connected by a master valve and a maintenance shaft.

She went to the panel and found the hidden release latch. She pulled the lever, the latch [#click]popped, and the panel opened. She crawled through the maintenance shaft to the other stasis bay, opened the hatch there, and squeezed inside.
A row of twelve silent pods appeared before her. She hurried down the line, scanning the small glowing nameplates.
At the end:

She was looking at Commander Astrid Vilde's face, pale and frozen in the blue gel. She had found her. Unbidden, the liquid around Vilde's face rippled. A vibration reached Joana, traveling through the floor plates beneath her feet. The sound swelled.

sections.heading: INDUSTRIAL PERIMETER · FREYA HOMESTEAD
paragraphs: Dawn over Mairee was a bruised purple with rivers of pale green phosphor. Oliver met Lysanne at the sector's loading gate. He carried only a rucksack. Lysanne had pulled her ruby hair back in a practical braid. It was tight enough to show the tension in her scalp.

paragraphs.text: "[annoyed]I can't believe you're late," she muttered, annoyed.
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]I'm sorry," he whispered back.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: He couldn't tell her the truth. He couldn't explain that he'd spent the last twenty minutes finalizing his Mechdroid Scrapper. He'd told himself he just needed to close the file. But the truth was a cold knot in his stomach. That if he hadn't finished it then, he might never get to.

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]We're actually doing this," she said.
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs: Oliver nodded. His throat was dry.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]The depot," he said, repeating what they had already agreed to, needing to hear it said aloud once more. "[steady]Close as you can get. You don't wait. You go straight to Joana."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: Lysanne's eyes flicked to him, sharp, checking.

paragraphs.text: "[checking]Go get the Egg and disappear before they catch you. No distractions."
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[steady]No detours."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: Oliver shifted the strap of his rucksack higher on his shoulder. The yard stretched out behind Lysanne, quiet machines beaded with condensation, cables slack, waiting for a signal to wake.

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Hey," Lysanne said, softer now, though it came out rough anyway. "[soft]No suicide run."
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]No."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: She held his gaze a moment longer. "[low]Good boy."
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs: They walked through the yard that held charging stations and parked vehicles. Their target was a flatbed transporter. They were twenty meters from it when a [#beam]white beam fell on them.

paragraphs.text: "[barking]Don't move!" a harsh voice barked.
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: Two guardians stepped out from behind the transporter.

paragraphs.text: "[barking]Identify yourselves."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs.text: *What are they still doing here? Did someone betray us?*

paragraphs: Oliver's heart raced.

paragraphs.text: Lysanne stepped forward slightly, her hands raised. "[steady]Lysanne Sutherland, Hydroponics. He's with me. We're —"
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs.text: "[interrupting]You're not on any roster for this sector. No one is," the guardian interrupted.
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: He was older, his face a canyon of cynical wrinkles. The second guardian, not much older than Oliver, twitched.

paragraphs.text: "[casual]We were taking a stroll," Oliver said, trying to sound casual. "[casual]A date."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: The older guardian gave a short, harsh laugh. "[harsh]A date. Cute."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: He stepped closer, his eyes lingering on Lysanne. He glanced at his twitchy teenage sidekick, who was clearly high on Zups.

paragraphs.text: "[leering]It's been a long shift, eh? Maybe we deserve a little fun too."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: He turned back to the two of them.

paragraphs.text: "[ordering]Get in the back. Both of you. We'll figure out what to do with you while we get this donkey to the docks."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: They had no choice. The younger guardian shoved Oliver toward the flatbed. He and Lysanne were pushed onto the cold metal deck. [#tarp]The older guardian threw a heavy, greasy tarp over the rails to cover them.

paragraphs.text: "[snarling]Stay quiet."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: They crawled into the dark. Oliver expected them to get into the cab and start driving, but the older man wasn't done yet.

paragraphs.text: "[idle]You know what's funny," he said, as if they had asked.
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: His eyes were on Lysanne.

paragraphs.text: "[idle]Not one new face will come through that Fold. Not one."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: He tilted his head.

paragraphs.text: "[calculating]Do the math. Three thousand of us. Most men. You know how many women your age?"
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: He gestured at Lysanne with the beam of his torch, moving up and down, slowly.
Lysanne said nothing. Her jaw was set.

paragraphs.text: "[answering himself]Not many," he answered himself.
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: He leaned forward.

paragraphs.text: "[predatory]No resupply. So, you can't waste a chance like this. More precious than water. More precious than fuel."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: His eyes moved over her with the flat assessment of a man pricing something at auction.
He nudged the younger one.

paragraphs.text: "[oily]Didn't the Centurion say so? Every able body is a colony asset. And isn't she a fine one."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: The younger guardian said nothing. His eyes went somewhere above the tarp. Somewhere far away. The older man rolled his shoulders. Cracked his neck.

paragraphs.text: "[purring]Now, about that date," he said, his voice a low, menacing purr. "[purring]Let's make it a memorable one. Suits. Off."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: Oliver shot Lysanne a look; her face was pale, but her eyes were fierce.

paragraphs.text: "[barking]Clothes!" the older guardian barked impatiently.
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs.text: "[urgent]Let them go," a voice cut in.
paragraphs.speaker: ian-perera

paragraphs: Ian Perera jumped up to the older guardian and, in one smooth sweep, pulled the guardian's pistol from its mag holster.
The older guardian stepped back, a wicked smile spreading across his wrinkled face.

paragraphs.text: "[smug]Well, well. Another stray dog."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: He reached [#baton-crackle]for his stun stick.

paragraphs.text: "[smug]That pistol is print-protected, Engineer."
paragraphs.speaker: older-guardian

paragraphs: Ian pulled the trigger, but the pistol didn't fire. Instead, he swung it at the older guardian's head, trying to use it as a club. The guardian caught Ian's wrist, twisted it, and [#baton-thwack]drove the stick into Ian's side.
Oliver jumped off the flatbed and swung his rucksack hard into the younger guardian's back. The guardian staggered. Lysanne followed right behind and kicked the back of the guardian's knee.

Oliver grabbed his multi-tool from his pocket and snapped out the blade. The younger guardian got back up and charged at Lysanne. Oliver darted forward and stabbed the guardian in the thigh. The guardian [#scream]screamed, but Lysanne cut him off with a sharp elbow to his throat. He fell to the ground, choking.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Go!" Ian shouted as he dropped to his knees, wincing while the elder guardian tried to grab the pistol back from him. "[shouting]Go! Now!"
paragraphs.speaker: ian-perera

paragraphs.text: Lysanne grabbed Oliver's arm, pulling him toward the cockpit of the transporter. "[urgent]He said go!"
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs: Oliver tossed his rucksack inside and climbed into the passenger seat. Lysanne climbed behind the wheel. The transporter [#engine-whine]groaned, and its coils whined as they came to life. She leaned out of the window to watch the fight behind her.
Ian lunged at the guardian, who had pulled his own gun. The [#gunshot]gun went off, and both men dropped to the ground.
For a heartbeat, neither moved. Oliver's hand was on the door latch.

The guardian got up, staggering to one knee. He still held the pistol, his arm covered in blood. He raised the weapon, the red targeting laser shaking, and started moving toward Ian to finish him off. Lysanne didn't hesitate. She [#scream]screamed with primal rage and slammed the throttle full reverse.
The guardian [#gunshot2]fired wildly as the transporter [#impact]slammed into him. There was a thud, and he disappeared beneath the vehicle.
Lysanne slammed the brake, and the transporter [#brake-screech]screeched to a stop.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Ian!" Oliver shouted as he jumped out.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: Lysanne hurried after him. They knelt beside Ian. He was on his back, teeth clenched in pain. A dark stain spread across his jumpsuit at the shoulder.

paragraphs.text: "[grunting]It went through," Ian grunted.
paragraphs.speaker: ian-perera

paragraphs: Oliver checked beneath the transporter for the groaning figure crushed beneath it and kicked away his gun. The younger guardian ran off. Lysanne tore open Ian's jumpsuit, searching for the wound.

paragraphs.text: "[panicking]We have to call this off," Oliver said, panicking. "[panicking]We have to get him to the Med-Post."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[weak laugh]Not a chance." Ian laughed weakly. "[weak]There's no going back now."
paragraphs.speaker: ian-perera

paragraphs: Lysanne used Oliver's multi-tool to tear a strip of fabric from her pack and wrapped it around Ian's shoulder, then yanked the pack's strap free and cinched it tight over the makeshift bandage. Together, they lifted Ian into the back of the transporter. Ian [#scream]screamed in pain.

paragraphs.text: "[clipped]Keep pressure on it," Lysanne instructed and climbed back into the driver's seat.
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland
